comments.text: I think $a_0 = 0$ should be added to the formulation, otherwise, we're quickly getting "no": Let $F(x):=\#\{(i,j): i\le j,\ a_i+a_j\le x\}$. For each $k$, set $f_k(n):=\#\{(i,j): 1\le i\le j\le k,\ a_i+a_j\le n\}$. By definition, $a_{k+1}$ is the smallest $n$ with $f_k(n)<n-k$. Hence: For $n=a_{k+1}-1$ we do not have $f_k(n)<n-k$, so $f_k(a_{k+1}-1)\ge (a_{k+1}-1)-k = a_{k+1}-k-1$. But at $n=a_{k+1}$ we do have $f_k(a_{k+1})<a_{k+1}-k$, so (since $f_k$ is integer-valued) $f_k(a_{k+1})\le a_{k+1}-k-1$. Therefore $f_k(a_{k+1})=a_{k+1}-k-1$. Now observe that at $x=a_{k+1}$, no pair involving $a_{k+1}$ can contribute, because $a_1+a_{k+1}=1+a_{k+1}>a_{k+1}$. So $F(a_{k+1})=f_k(a_{k+1})$, and thus $F(a_{k+1})=a_{k+1}-k-1$. Equivalently, $F(a_m)=a_m-m$ for $m\ge 2$. In particular, the error $|F(a_m)-a_m|=m$ occurs infinitely often (for every $m$). Also, $a_m-m = F(a_m)\le \#\{(i,j):1\le i\le j\le m-1\}=\frac{(m-1)m}{2}$, so $a_m\ll m^2$, i.e. $m\gg \sqrt{a_m}$. Hence $|F(a_m)-a_m| = m \gg \sqrt{a_m}$, so $F(x)=x+O(x^{1/4+o(1)})$ is impossible. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

Aha, good catch, thanks. It is odd since Erdős explicitly considers the alternative definition in which $a_0=0$ and the condition is that the number of solutions of $a_i+a_j\leq x$ with $0\leq i\leq j\leq k$ (and $j>0$) is $<x$. I guess that Rosen described it to Erdős in the latter form, and Erdős noted that these two constructions produce the same sequence, but did not notice that the count of $a_i+a_j\leq x$ in the conclusion is also changed when switching between the two.

I submitted A390642. Until it gets published you can see draft edits here. If the main sequence is f(x) and the number of a(i)+a(j)<=x is g(x) then f(x)=g(x)+x. Here are the first few terms of each seq: f(x): 1, 3, 5, 9, 13, 17, 24, 31, 38, 45, 53, 61, 75, 87, 97, 112, 124, 139, 147, 175, 182, 205, 219, 242, 265, 277, 309, 313, 349, 378... g(x): 1, 1, 2, 5, 8, 11, 17, 23, 29, 35, 42, 49, 62, 73, 82, 96, 107, 121, 128, 155, 161, 183, 196, 218, 240, 251, 282, 285, 320, 348... Please feel free to delete this message (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
